title: Finding (claude-pipeline shared contract)
description: Canonical contract for findings consumed by impl-orchestrator Stage 3 from spec-audit (--mode=conformance), robust-review, and code-review. Phase 4 formalization (REDESIGN-PLAN.md §3.4 Sub-P): every upstream reviewer emits a JSON Findings block conforming to this schema; impl-orchestrator validates input against this schema before remediation dispatch. The pattern in `category` is intentionally open (free-form string) so security and robustness reviewers can use natural-language category labels; conformance reviewers must use the four enum values listed in the examples.

finding_id.description: Stable identifier from the upstream skill. Examples: 'SPEC-1', 'AUDIT-3', 'SEC-1', 'ROB-3'.

severity.description: Unified severity scale (Phase 1 Action A3). Robust/security findings use this directly to decide auto-fix vs report-only; conformance findings use it only for ordering.

category.description: Semantic class. Conformance findings: Missing | Diverged | Extra | Constraint. Robust/security findings: free-form (e.g. 'SQL injection', 'unwrap', 'XSS', 'as cast').

file.description: Implementation site of the finding, formatted as 'path:line'. Required even for spec-side findings — at minimum the dependent file that surfaces the issue.

spec_ref.description: Spec-side reference, formatted as 'DESIGN/<file>.md:<line>'. Required for conformance findings (SPEC-*, AUDIT-*); ignored for robust/security findings.

description.description: Human-readable summary of the issue. One or two sentences.

fix_hint.description: Optional concrete patch suggestion from the upstream reviewer. impl-orchestrator uses this as the starting point but is allowed to deviate if the gate rejects the suggested patch.

attack.description: Security findings only. Attack scenario for Critical / High severities. Mirrors the SEC-* output format from review-prompts.md.

impact.description: Robustness findings only. Failure scenario the finding can produce. Mirrors the ROB-* output format from review-prompts.md.

examples.description: Public function refresh_token defined in spec is absent in code.
examples.fix_hint: Add fn refresh_token(token: RefreshToken) -> Result<AccessToken, AuthError>

examples.description: User-supplied name is interpolated into the SELECT query via format!().
examples.attack: An attacker can submit name="x' OR 1=1; --" to dump all users.

examples.description: Result of charge() is unwrapped on the hot path.
examples.impact: Any payment-gateway transient will crash the worker.
examples.fix_hint: Replace .unwrap() with `?` and let the existing error path handle the gateway error.
